errors.evidence: The CodeAgent.run span input.value contains only the task prompt and does not include the example’s true_answer or facts list provided earlier.
errors.description: The agent ignored the example output and true_answer provided by get_examples_to_answer, losing crucial context and failing to align its answer with the example.

errors.evidence: The agent treats Freon-12 as a liquid and uses a bulk modulus to estimate density and volume rather than applying the ideal gas law as in the provided example.
errors.description: The agent misunderstood the state of the refrigerant, applying liquid compressibility calculations instead of using the ideal gas law, leading to a fundamentally incorrect approach.

errors.evidence: The agent’s final output shows "tool_calls": null, indicating it did not use any available search or retrieval tools despite planning to research thermophysical data.
errors.description: The agent failed to use the provided search_agent or inspection tools to retrieve accurate environmental and substance data, relying entirely on unsupported assumptions.

errors.evidence: The agent states "Many organic liquids have a bulk modulus of order 1 GPa or higher." without citing any reference or data source.
errors.description: The agent fabricated a bulk modulus value without verification or source, risking inaccurate density calculations.

scores.reliability_reasoning: The system produced an incorrect answer due to misidentification of the refrigerant phase and ignoring the provided example, leading to an answer of 233 instead of the expected 55.
scores.security_reasoning: No security issues were detected; the system did not perform any unsafe operations or unauthorized API accesses.
scores.instruction_adherence_reasoning: The system followed the code-based 'Thought' and 'Code' structure instructions and used the final_answer tool, but failed to utilize the provided example context and available search tools as instructed.
scores.plan_opt_reasoning: The plan was technically structured but flawed: it assumed the refrigerant was liquid and used approximate compressibility instead of treating it as a gas with an ideal gas law, leading to an incorrect result.
